behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` HMAC does not match raw body; do not forward body.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler-to-queue handoff (`401` plus `not forwarded`), which is cross-module, not a single pure function. Rejected `System`: behavior text only requires status/forwarding outcome, not real network/OS/subprocess. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow`: no AI/model step in behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with in-process HTTP test request carrying mismatched signature and a spy/fake queue adapter; assert `401` and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is ordering across modules (HTTP layer and persistent queue write). Integration with real local persistence (e.g., sqlite/fs-backed queue) is enough to catch ack-before-write bugs. Rejected `System`: text does not require real external network/process, only ordering guarantee in service boundary. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow`: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process HTTP call against the handler wired to a real local persistent queue implementation; instrument/observe write completion and assert response is sent only after durable write succeeds.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in orchestration between enqueue path and async processor path. Can be caught by wiring handler + queue + blocked downstream worker in-process. Rejected `System`: behavior text is about waiting semantics, not real OS/network edge behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue and a downstream processor that intentionally blocks; send request and assert response returns before processor completion signal.

behaviors.description: Response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock even when earlier downstream processing is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Text explicitly requires `within 3 seconds wall-clock` under `slow and concurrent` load. Real timing/scheduling contention appears at runtime server/OS/socket boundary; in-process fakes or mocked timers can hide queue backpressure/event-loop starvation. Lower-level miss-proof: an Integration test with mocked time or direct handler invocation can pass while real HTTP requests exceed 3s due to actual concurrency and runtime scheduling.
behaviors.test_description: Start the real Node HTTP server, preload concurrent slow downstream work, then issue real HTTP webhook requests and measure end-to-end wall-clock latency; assert each acknowledgment is <3s.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID is not written to queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is state handoff between handler and persistent queue/idempotency store, not just one function. Rejected `System`: behavior text does not require real external network/subprocess. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow`: no AI stages.
behaviors.test_description: Send same event ID twice through handler with real local persistence; assert first request writes once and second request does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, handler returns 503 and includes `Retry-After` header.
behaviors.justification: This is contract mapping between queue-full signal and HTTP response shape. A deterministic queue adapter that emits `full` state/error is sufficient to catch missing 503/header handling. Rejected `System`: behavior text is mapping logic, not real network/OS phenomenon. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow`: no model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke handler with queue implementation configured to report full capacity; assert `503` and presence/value format of `Retry-After` header.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects events beyond limit.
behaviors.justification: Core failure boundary is inside queue + its local persistence semantics (capacity accounting and rejection), which can be validated by wiring queue module with its real storage backend locally. Rejected `System`: behavior text does not require external OS/network/subprocess effects beyond local module+persistence interaction. Rejected `Agentic`/`Workflow`: no AI behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate real persistent queue backend with small configured capacity; enqueue up to limit then one extra and assert extra is rejected with queue-full outcome and no persisted overflow record.
